snp4.6:1.2: You die this side of a century,
snp4.6:1.3: but even if you live past,
snp4.6:2.1: People grieve for what they see as mine,
snp4.6:2.2: for nothing possessed is constant, nothing is constantly possessed.
snp4.6:2.3: Seeing this separation simply as it is,
snp4.6:2.4: one shouldn’t follow the household life.
snp4.6:3.4: shouldn’t incline to be devoted to mine-ness.
snp4.6:4.1: Just as a man doesn’t see on awakening
snp4.6:4.2: what he met in a dream,
snp4.6:4.3: even so he doesn’t see, when they are dead
snp4.6:4.4: —their time done— those he held dear.
snp4.6:5.2: people are called by this name or that,
snp4.6:5.4: to be pointed to when they are dead.
snp4.6:6.2: are not let go by those greedy for mine,
snp4.6:6.4: letting go of possessions, go about seeing the Secure.
snp4.6:7.3: They say it’s congenial for him,
snp4.6:7.4: he who wouldn’t, in any realm, display self.
snp4.6:8.4: like water on a white lotus, do not adhere.
snp4.6:9.1: As a water bead on a lotus leaf,
snp4.6:9.2: as water on a red lily, doesn’t adhere,
snp4.6:9.4: to the seen, the heard, or the sensed;
snp4.6:10.2: in connection with the seen, the heard, or the sensed.
snp4.6:10.3: In no other way does he wish for purity,
snp4.6:10.4: for he neither takes on passion nor puts it away.
